snow_or_rain_notes: Spring (April-May): snow present closer to Viewpoint 1; snowfall possible at higher campsites like Dzongri after rain on lower slopes; patches of snow at higher altitude
Autumn (October-November): no snow at higher campsites
Spring: rain on lower slopes can trigger snowfall at higher campsites like Dzongri
temperature_notes: Spring (April-May): daytime 12°C to 15°C; nighttime 0°C to -3°C; 3 warm layers required
Autumn (October-November): daytime 8°C to 12°C; nighttime -5°C to -10°C; 5 warm layers required

terrain_challenges: Approximately 9,260 ft total altitude gain (5,840 ft to 15,100 ft)
Daily ascents, descents and boulder sections on steep, uneven trails
Most days involve longer trekking durations
Wooden path section from Tshoka into rhododendron forests
altitude_or_weather_risks: Difficulty in breathing expected from Tshoka to Dzongri via Phedang
Phedang/Dzongri are points where trek leader can send a trekker back for AMS symptoms or lack of fitness
Rapid altitude increase makes trekkers very susceptible to AMS
At 13,190 ft at Dzongri on Day 3, climbers have gone too high too soon
Above 10,000 ft, AMS risk is real for anyone including first-time and experienced trekkers

fitness_benchmark: Walk, jog or run 10 km in under 60 minutes (minimum recommended fitness level)
approval_requirements: Every trekker needs fitness approval from the Indiahikes team 20 days before the trek date; without it you will not be allowed on the trek
Record at least 3 runs from the last 1 month to meet the fitness criteria
Use apps like Nike and Strava to record runs; paste your public profile link on your Indiahikes account
Fitness record may be rejected if you fail to link your fitness profile or do not meet the required fitness level at least three times in the past month
prep_window: 6 to 8 weeks before your trek begins
6-week plan to get trek fit if you are not currently active

experience_themes: Spring: blooming rhododendrons in varied shades (pink, scarlet, yellow, white) along the trail from Bakhim through Tshoka to Phedang, forming a rhododendron tunnel
Spring: misty forests with romantic, dreamy look
Spring: possible snowfall at higher campsites like Dzongri after rain on lower slopes
Autumn: dark mossy forest under brilliant blue autumn sky with deep reds and browns
Autumn: sharp, clear views from Tshoka, Dzongri, Thansing and ViewPoint 1
Autumn: piercing star-lit sky, good for night-sky photography
primary_watchouts: Spring: less snow than comparable treks in Uttarakhand or Himachal Pradesh
Autumn: no rhododendrons; colder nights than spring
Approximately 9,260 ft total altitude gain (5,840 ft to 15,100 ft)
Daily ascents, descents and boulder sections on steep, uneven trails
Most days involve longer trekking durations
best_for: Spring: witnessing blooming rhododendrons and a romantic, dreamy forest experience
Autumn: sharp mountain views and night-sky photography
